name: Al Fresco
classification_notes: A casual, flowing script with looped lowercase letterforms, swash alternates and stylistic sets characteristic of hand-lettered casual scripts used in packaging and titling.
summary: A casual looped script in two weights, built with extensive OpenType features including 157 swash forms, 46 decorative ligatures and titling alternates that strip the loops for a cleaner look.
description: Designed for packaging and titling, Al Fresco offers a breezy handwritten character with loops on lowercase letters that can be removed via titling capitals for a more contemporary aesthetic. Contextual alternates reduce ending strokes to give a more naturalistic, handcrafted feel. The OpenType set includes two extra uppercase alphabets, 16 ornaments and special coding to access all alternates without professional software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Al Fresco as a breezy, light and expressive typeface suited to packaging and titling. Activating titling removes loops from lowercase letters for a cleaner, more contemporary look, while contextual alternates minimise ending strokes to reveal the warmth and uniqueness of the human hand. The family includes 157 lowercase swash forms, 46 decorative ligatures, 16 ornaments and two additional uppercase alphabets.

name: Alana
classification_notes: A connected, flowing script with casual elegance and ink-on-paper texture; the letterforms join continuously and include swash capitals and ornamental alternates typical of the script category.
summary: A connected script with ink-textured strokes, over 300 alternates including swash capitals, and 62 botanical and illustrative ornaments.
description: Designed for settings ranging from menu details to display headlines, the typeface shifts character depending on size: at small sizes the letterforms read with casual warmth, while at display sizes rough ink edges become visible. The family includes three styles — Regular, Regular Smooth and Bold — and ships with swash capitals, ornamented forms and a set of botanical fleurons, birds and decorative bugs coded for access without professional design software. Latin Extended coverage spans 27 languages including Central and Eastern European scripts.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Alana as a connected script that glows with casual elegance, suited to letter-writing, menu details and packaging. At display sizes its strokes reveal rough edges evocative of ink on textured paper, and the family includes over 300 alternates plus 62 matching ornaments — botanical fleurons, birds and small decorative bugs — all accessible without professional design software.

name: Adorn
classification_notes: A large, mixed-style decorative collection spanning hand-lettered scripts, copperplate, engraved display, serif, slab serif, sans serif and ornament fonts — best classified as display due to its primary purpose in celebratory and decorative contexts.
summary: A sprawling 41-style collection of hand-lettered and decorative typefaces built for wedding stationery, invitations and celebratory design.
description: Spanning scripts, copperplate, engraved display faces, serifs, slab serifs, condensed and expanded sans serifs, monograms, banners, frames and ornaments, Adorn is designed to be mixed and matched within a single project. Each sub-family ships in a standard and Smooth variant, the latter suited to lower-resolution output. OpenType features including multiple stylistic sets, swashes, titling capitals and contextual alternates are accessible without specialist software via coded shortcuts.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington positions Adorn as a warm, welcoming alternative to the staid look of formal invitations, combining a modern and sometimes quirky sensibility with a large range of harmonious fonts. The collection includes seven display fonts, four script designs, monograms, ornaments, illustrations, banners, frames and catchwords, all built to work together despite each sub-family's distinctiveness.

name: Alisha
classification_notes: A flowing connected script with varied letterforms, generous x-height and extensive swash and alternate forms, characteristic of expressive hand-lettering styles.
summary: A connected script with a generous x-height and over 200 alternates and swash forms, aimed at packaging, headlines and identity work.
description: Alisha draws on the character of custom hand lettering, with varied letterforms that give it a personalised quality without sacrificing legibility. More than 200 stylistic alternates, swash forms and ornaments are accessible via OpenType features, and the font is coded to work without professional design software. Coverage spans Latin Extended, supporting a broad range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Alisha as an expressive yet legible script with a generous x-height and over 200 alternates and swash forms. She positions it as ideal for product packaging, headlines, hero graphics and identity work, noting that the letterforms retain a personable, custom-lettering quality while remaining polished and refined.

name: Beloved
classification_notes: An all-capitals display sans with extensive swash and alternate forms, Art Deco titling variants, and accompanying script and ornament families, placing it firmly in decorative display territory.
summary: An all-capitals display sans with over 800 swash and alternate forms, sold alongside a matching script and ornament family for wordmarks and decorative headlines.
description: Beloved Sans is an all-capitals face with small capitals scaled to roughly 85% of the full caps, offering just enough contrast for use in display settings. Its 800-plus swash and alternate forms make it well-suited to wordmarks and decorative headlines, with titling variants nodding to Art Deco and swashed capitals adding calligraphic character. The collection bundles Beloved Script in regular and bold, plus a Design Elements family of ornaments, all coded to expose alternates without requiring professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Beloved Sans as a simple sans that likes to get swashy, noting its all-capitals construction, small capitals scaled to around 85%, and more than 800 swash and alternate forms for distinctive wordmarks and headlines. The titling variant adds Art Deco flavour, and the fonts are coded to make alternates and ornaments accessible without specialist software.

name: Azalea
classification_notes: A brush script with inky, hand-lettered strokes referencing 1950s brush lettering traditions, featuring organic thicks and thins with a jaunty, angular quality.
summary: A brush script drawing on 1950s hand-lettering traditions, offered in rough and smooth variants with a companion ornaments set.
description: Inky strokes and artfully irregular thicks and thins give this brush script an organic, hand-lettered character rooted in mid-century lettering styles. The family splits into Rough and Smooth variants, each with a matching ornaments font, while alternate capital letters in the Rough cut lean further into a casual, rustic quality. OpenType features include stylistic alternates, stylistic sets and titling capitals, with the fonts coded to allow access to swashes and ornaments without professional layout software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Azalea as drawing on classic brush script styles of the 1950s, with a jaunty angularity that reads as distinctly modern. Artfully irregular thicks and thins lend it an organic feel, and the rough-edged version is positioned as a rustic complement to the smooth cut, with an alternate set of more casual capital letters.

name: Bianca
classification_notes: Bianca is explicitly described as a semi-connected script with slim strokes and gently curving verticals, placing it firmly in the script category with a calligraphic, fashion-oriented style.
summary: A slim, semi-connected script with elegant capitals and over 50 alternate glyphs, suited to fashion and lifestyle branding.
description: Slim strokes, gently curving verticals and large decorative capitals give Bianca a refined, feminine character typical of fashion-oriented display scripts. A single Regular weight is available, but over 50 alternate glyphs accessible via OpenType features allow considerable variation within a design. Latin Extended coverage spans 27 languages including Czech, Polish and Turkish.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Bianca as a semi-connected script whose slim strokes, gently curving verticals and big, beautiful capitals convey a fashionable, feminine style. The font ships with over 50 alternate glyphs for customisation and has been specially coded so that swashes, alternates and ornaments are accessible without professional design software.

name: Blithe
classification_notes: A monoline casual script mimicking felt-tip handwriting with irregular letterforms, ligatures and alternates typical of informal handwritten scripts.
summary: A monoline casual script based on felt-tip handwriting, with 148 ligatures and 47 alternates to simulate natural variation.
description: Blithe draws on the loose, imperfect quality of felt-tip pen handwriting, using clean monoline strokes and deliberately varied letterforms to avoid mechanical uniformity. The character set includes 148 ligatures and 47 alternates, giving designers the tools to produce convincing, flowing connections across a wide range of Latin-extended languages. Suited to social media graphics, food packaging and children's products where an approachable, gentle tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Blithe as capturing the casual flair of a felt-tip pen through clean monoline strokes and the distinctive quirks of real handwriting, including imperfectly varied letterforms. The font ships with 148 ligatures and 47 alternates for authentic-looking connections, and has been coded to allow access to all alternates and swashes without professional design software. It is positioned for uses such as Instagram posts, cupcake menus and baby clothing.

name: Boucherie
classification_notes: A collection of four distinct display typefaces inspired by 19th-century French advertising typography, including block, cursive, flared and sans subfamilies, clearly placing it in the display category with a decorative, historical vernacular character.
summary: A coordinated collection of display typefaces drawn from 19th-century French advertising typography, with four complementary subfamilies and a suite of ornamental design elements.
description: Four distinct display fonts cover block, cursive, flared and sans styles, all designed to sit together in typographic compositions that recall French commercial lettering of the 1800s. Catchwords, frames and ornaments extend the system for more elaborate layouts. OpenType swashes, alternates and stylistic sets are accessible without professional design software, via coded shortcuts built into the fonts.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Boucherie as capturing the lively essence of 19th-century French advertising typography through a collection of original designs. She frames it as a solution to the perennial challenge of finding fonts that work together to evoke a particular era, offering four complementary display fonts alongside ornaments, catchwords and frames for typographic compositions that feel at once fresh and familiar.

name: Buckley
classification_notes: A condensed, ink-textured hand-lettered script based on fountain pen strokes, with randomised contextual alternates to simulate natural handwriting variation.
summary: A condensed, fountain-pen-derived script with ink-on-paper texture and randomised alternates to simulate natural handwriting.
description: Drawn from characters written with a Waterman Ideal #2 fountain pen, Buckley carries visible ink texture and a condensed stance that gives it warmth without formality. Randomised contextual alternates cycle through multiple letterforms as you type, reducing the repetition that makes hand-lettered fonts read as mechanical. It suits packaging, greeting cards and children's publishing where an approachable, informal tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Buckley as a condensed, hand-lettered font based on characters created with her favourite vintage fountain pen, a Waterman Ideal #2. Its textured strokes reveal ink-on-paper origins, while randomised contextual alternates cycle through letterforms for a convincing handwritten appearance. The foundry notes its friendly, happy and casual character makes it suitable for packaging, greetings and storybooks.

name: Caprizant
classification_notes: An upright pointed-pen script, designed for display use with extensive swash and alternate glyph sets. Classified as script rather than display due to its clear calligraphic structure and letterform construction.
summary: An upright pointed-pen script with 337 swashes, three capital sets and contextual alternates for display and identity use.
description: Built on letterforms inked with a pointed pen, Caprizant sits upright rather than leaning into a traditional script slant, giving it a lively formality suited to titles and logotypes. The two-weight family is bolstered by 337 swashes, 20 ornaments, dozens of ligatures and alternates that toggle between fully connected and unconnected appearances. Laura Worthington has coded the fonts to expose all these features without requiring professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Caprizant is described by the foundry as a lively, upright script based on pointed-pen lettering, built for display settings, titles and identity work. It offers 337 swashes, three sets of capitals, 20 ornaments, full-letter alternates, ligatures and contextual alternates, with special coding to make all features accessible outside professional design applications.

name: Charcuterie
classification_notes: A large multi-style display collection spanning block, serif, sans, cursive, engraved and deco styles alongside ornamental elements, all designed for headline and titling use rather than extended text.
summary: A ten-typeface display collection with matching ornaments, built for mixing across headlines, packaging, signage and logo work.
description: Spanning block, serif, sans, cursive, flared, deco and engraved styles, the family is designed to be used in combination as much as individually. Swash alternates number in the hundreds across individual subfamilies, and the Design Elements subset adds catchwords, frames and over 100 ornamental glyphs. The collection suits a craft or artisanal visual register without being tied to a single historical reference.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Charcuterie as a collection of ten distinct yet related typefaces and three ornamental typefaces suited to headlines, titling, logos, display, packaging, signage and advertising. She frames the entire collection as a complete and complex toolbox enabling experimentation, with highlights including 135 swash alternates in Engraved, 275 in Cursive, and an Ornaments set of 100 glyphs covering arrows, brackets, rules, icons and ribbons.

name: Elaina
classification_notes: Elaina Script is a connected cursive script modelled closely on the designer's own handwriting, with restrained ascenders and descenders and a sober x-height — placing it firmly in the formal/penned script tradition. The companion SemiSerif is a complementary roman for body contexts.
summary: A tidily penned connected script drawn closely from Laura Worthington's own handwriting, paired with a semi-serif companion for body text.
description: Elaina Script keeps a sober x-height with restrained ascenders and descenders, making it readable at smaller display sizes and in short text passages. An unconnected alternate set for the lowercase gives flexibility, while hundreds of swashes, discretionary ligatures and stylistic sets cater to larger-scale logotype and brand identity work. The collection includes Elaina SemiSerif, an upright companion designed for body copy and complementary settings.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Elaina Script as the typeface closest of all her designs to her own handwriting. In standard form its measured proportions suit smaller display sizes and short text blocks; its stylistic alternates provide an unconnected lowercase, and it ships with the full complement of swashes, alternates and ligatures she is known for. Elaina SemiSerif accompanies it as a body text and complementary companion.

name: Ed's Market
classification_notes: The family is built on hand-lettered brush and script styles rooted in 20th-century showcard and grocery poster lettering, placing it firmly in the script category with a brush display subcategory.
summary: A hand-lettered brush and script family drawing on 20th-century grocery poster and showcard lettering traditions, with three letter variations per character to avoid mechanical repetition.
description: Each weight and variant in the family was individually hand-lettered rather than derived computationally, giving the seven brush display faces and three script styles a consistent but genuinely varied texture. Smart OpenType programming cycles through three alternates per character so repeated letters do not betray themselves. Supplementary Design Elements glyphs supply arrows, ribbons, badges and other period signage ornaments to complete the aesthetic.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Ed's Market as being like having your own professional sign painter with a solid repertoire of styles: each one distinctive, yet clearly by the same hand. No variants were created on the computer; every weight was individually hand-lettered to evoke the warm, inviting feel of classic 20th-century grocery posters and showcard lettering. Three letter variations per character ensure a natural hand-painted look when characters repeat, while over 100 alternates and swash forms are included across the script styles.

name: Congenial
classification_notes: Congenial is a sans-serif with calligraphic underpinnings visible in the heavier weights, giving it the warmth and character differentiation associated with humanist sans designs rather than geometric or grotesque models.
summary: A 10-weight humanist sans by Laura Worthington, designed as a legible everyday companion to her decorative display library, with calligraphic traces most visible in the heavier cuts.
description: Designed primarily for use on Worthington's own website, Congenial draws on her calligraphic background to introduce subtle hand-drawn character into an otherwise clean sans-serif framework. The family spans Hairline to Black across 20 styles including italics throughout, and features a generous x-height with carefully differentiated letterforms for strong legibility. OpenType features include small capitals, old-style numerals, fractions and superscript, with broad Latin language coverage across European scripts.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington designed Congenial as an understated, highly legible sans-serif to complement her decorative display faces, never straying far from her calligraphic roots. The result is a friendly and inviting face with a generous x-height and highly differentiated characters, spanning 10 weights with matching italics.

name: Ganache
classification_notes: Ganache is described as a hybrid of script, Roman and italic forms, making it a display script with calligraphic qualities. Its blend of script and upright letterforms places it firmly in the display script category.
summary: A single-weight hybrid script that blends upright Roman, italic and cursive letterforms with 185 swashes and alternates.
description: Ganache merges script, Roman and italic traditions into one display face, with uppercase letters that treat negative space between characters as a form of active swash. The family ships with 185 swashes and alternates plus 10 ornaments, and Laura Worthington has coded the font so these extras are accessible without professional design software. Latin Extended coverage spans around 25 Western European languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Ganache as a smart, intricate, fun and deceptively simple font — a distinctive hybrid of script, Roman and italic that turns letter-fitting and negative space into an integral part of the design. Boldly stylish uppercase letters feature unexpected counter shapes, and the family offers 185 swashes, alternates and 10 ornaments.

name: Funkydori
classification_notes: A decorative display face explicitly designed as a homage to 1970s graphic lettering, with swash capitals, ornaments and expressive alternates that place it firmly in the display category with a retro subcategory.
summary: A two-weight display face modelled on 1970s lettering, loaded with swash capitals, ornaments and multiple stylistic alternates for expressive typesetting.
description: Built as a tribute to 1970s graphic aesthetics, the family comes in Regular and Bold with an extensive set of OpenType features including swash capitals, titling alternates, discretionary ligatures and 38 ornaments and patterns. Multiple stylistic sets give designers granular control over letterform variants without requiring professional design software. Suited to packaging, editorial and branding work that wants a knowingly retro character.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Funkydori as a typographic homage to the groovy 1970s, updated for 21st-century design. Drawing on her own childhood memories of the era, she created bodacious letterforms enhanced with extravagant swash capitals, alternates and endings, complemented by 38 ornaments and patterns. The fonts are specially coded to allow access to all swashes, alternates and ornaments without needing professional design software.

name: Fairwater
classification_notes: The family centres on a connected cursive script inspired by early-to-mid 20th-century handwriting and tattoo lettering, which defines its primary classification; the accompanying sans and decorative serifs are supporting members of the collection.
summary: A nostalgic script family drawn from early 20th-century cursive handwriting and naval tattoo lettering, bundled with a matching sans, decorative serifs and an extensive ornamental font.
description: The collection spans a connected script in three weights, each loaded with 465 swashes and alternates including ending forms, isolated letters and contextual variants, alongside a three-weight sans and four decorative serif cuts. A separate ornament font, DIY Lines, contributes 250 characters of ships, anchors, rope, botanicals and arrows that share stroke weight and proportions with the type. The pictorial themes and simplified letterforms lean clearly toward vintage Americana and maritime design work.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Fairwater as drawing from cursive handwriting styles popularised in the early to mid-1900s, the simplified letterforms of tattoo lettering, and the pictorial themes of early 20th-century naval tattoos. The family includes a script and sans in three weights, four decorative serif faces and an ornamental font with 250 characters of maritime and botanical motifs, all specially coded to access alternates and swashes without professional design software.

name: Greeting Cards
classification_notes: A hand-lettered script font built around pre-drawn greeting expressions rather than composable letterforms, placing it firmly in the decorative script category.
summary: A hand-lettered font built around over 30 pre-drawn greeting expressions, each offered in single-line and multi-line variants for quick use in cards and digital publications.
description: Rather than a conventional alphabetic script, this font packages hand-lettered phrases as individual glyphs, each expression available in both a single-line and a stacked multi-line version. Transparent GIFs of all expressions are included alongside the font, making it straightforward to drop them into emails or digital layouts. The approach suits quick, expressive work where consistent lettering is more useful than typesetting flexibility.
foundry_blurb: Greeting Cards contains over 30 hand-lettered expressions designed with versatility in mind. Most multi-word greetings come in both a single-line version and an alternate with words across two or more lines. A bonus folder of transparent GIFs is included for easy use in emails and publications.

name: Grindel Grove
classification_notes: A single-weight display face with bark-like texture and pointed v-shaped letterforms designed explicitly for spooky, atmospheric use. Its ornamental and illustrative character places it firmly in the display/decorative category.
summary: A single-weight display face with bark-textured letterforms and pointed strokes, suited to horror, Halloween and gothic-themed design work.
description: The typeface draws on the visual language of dark forests and cautionary folklore, with v-shaped letterforms and a rough, bark-like surface that gives it an unsettling handcrafted quality. Available in one weight with stylistic alternates, it covers a broad Latin character set including Central and Northern European languages. Best deployed at large sizes on book covers, event posters or branded material where a spooky atmosphere is the explicit goal.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Grindel Grove as a spooky display face that evokes deep dark woods, lost treasure maps and cautionary fables. Its bark-like texture and v-shaped letterforms lend an air of eerie mystery, making it a fit for scary novels, haunted houses and strange happenings.

name: Gioviale
classification_notes: Gioviale is explicitly described as a hybrid of italic and script, combining the fluidity of a connected script with the structured underpinnings of an italic, placing it firmly in the script category with an italic-script subcategory.
summary: A hybrid italic-script with calligraphic flair, notable for its readability at small sizes and an unusually deep set of swash and alternate characters.
description: Combining the flow of a connected script with the structured discipline of an italic, Gioviale offers better legibility at small sizes than most scripts in its class. The family comes in two weights, Regular and Bold, and is furnished with 300 swashes, 20 ornaments, 20 discretionary ligatures, alternate swash capitals and 16 stylistic sets. Latin Extended language support covers over two dozen European languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington Design describes Gioviale as a unique hybrid blending an italic and script typeface with flair and elegance, underpinned by tailored, clean and structured qualities. The foundry highlights its greater readability at small sizes compared to other scripts, and notes its extensive OpenType feature set, including swash caps, ornaments, discretionary ligatures and 300 swashes, all accessible without the need for professional design software.

name: Hummingbird
classification_notes: Hummingbird is a connected handwritten script drawing directly from cursive penmanship traditions, with multiple contextual alternates and swash sets to simulate natural handwriting variation.
summary: A connected cursive script with 214 contextual alternates and 266 swashes, designed to replicate the organic irregularity of traditional penmanship.
description: Modelled on old-fashioned cursive penmanship, the letterforms evoke bundled correspondence and leather-bound ledgers. The large inventory of contextual alternates prevents repetition across longer settings, while an extensive swash set includes a full suite of decorated uppercase letters. Specially encoded for use without professional design software, making the OpenType features accessible to a broader audience.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Hummingbird as reminiscent of old-fashioned cursive penmanship found in treasured letters or worn leather-bound ledgers, featuring 214 contextual alternates for flowing randomness and a more natural handwritten appearance, plus 266 swashes including an entire set of decorated uppercase letters.

name: Honey Bee
classification_notes: An all-caps decorative face with hand-lettered qualities, multiple swash and titling capital sets, and ornaments — firmly in the display category with no text use intended.
summary: An all-caps hand-lettered display face with multiple capital sets, swash alternates and 20 ornaments, aimed at food packaging, fantasy and children's design.
description: A single-weight all-caps display face drawn in a hand-lettered style, offering three sets of full-size capitals and two sets of smaller capitals to mix across headlines, titles and wordmarks. Twenty decorative ornaments are bundled alongside OpenType stylistic sets and titling alternates, all accessible without professional design software. Best suited to sweet or whimsical visual identities, packaging and editorial work for children's or food brands.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Honey Bee as an all-caps display face in a delightfully hand-lettered style, suited to food packaging, fantasy games and children's products. The font includes three sets of full-size capitals, two sets of smaller capitals, swash and titling forms for mixing in headlines and wordmarks, plus 20 ornaments — all specially coded for access without professional design software.

name: Harlean
classification_notes: A hand-lettered script with variable axis, baseline and height, evoking flexible-nib pen calligraphy and improvisational lettering; clearly a casual script category.
summary: A single-weight casual script drawing on flexible-nib pen lettering, with eccentric letterforms that vary in axis, baseline and height for an improvisational feel.
description: Built around the rhythms of flexible-nib pen calligraphy, Harlean uses contextual alternates to generate convincing hand-lettered variation across words. Swashes, stylistic alternates and titling capitals give room for customisation, while ornaments, borders and corner elements extend its usefulness for decorative layout work. The OpenType features are coded to work without professional design software, broadening its accessible appeal.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Harlean as lively and exciting, with eccentric letterforms that vary in axis, baseline and height as a tribute to its flexible-nib pen origins. Its curving strokes speak of free-spirited adventure and a jazz-inflected, improvisational style, with hand-lettered appearance achieved through contextual alternates and a suite of swashes, alternates, ornaments, borders and corner elements.

name: Intermingle
classification_notes: Intermingle is described as a script design pared down to essential forms with swash elements, placing it firmly in the script category as a casual or brush-influenced script.
summary: A deliberately simplified script with three weights and a variable font, designed by Laura Worthington for packaging, branding and editorial use.
description: Stripped back to its most essential forms, Intermingle balances practical legibility with selective swash detailing. Available in Regular, Bold and Black with a variable font included, it was inspired by Worthington's own Funkydori and Ganache, and is designed to work alongside either. Latin Extended language support covers over 25 languages across Central and Northern European scripts.
foundry_blurb: Intermingle is a script design intentionally pared down to the most essential forms, giving designers a more versatile typeface with the right balance of practical subtlety and eye-catching swash. Inspired by the bold simplicity of Funkydori and Ganache, it can be used alongside either family for a richer typographic palette, and suits packaging, branding, magazines and books. Available in 3 weights or as a variable font.

name: Ladybird
classification_notes: Ladybird has the playful, rounded letterforms and arcing strokes of a casual script or informal display face, with ligatures and alternates typical of decorative script-adjacent designs rather than a traditional upright category.
summary: A compact, playful display face with arcing ascenders, curved descenders and a collection of ligatures and alternates suited to informal and youth-oriented settings.
description: Ladybird is a single-weight display face with rounded, cosy letterforms and characteristic arcing ascenders with curled descenders that give it a cheerful, lightweight feel. Common ligatures and stylistic alternates add variety and a sense of spontaneity across text. The font is coded to allow access to alternates and ornaments without professional design software, broadening its accessibility for casual users.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Ladybird as an unabashedly playful face for joyful, summery and youth-oriented settings, noting that its gently arcing ascenders and cutely curved descenders grow from cosy, compact letterforms, with a sprinkling of ligatures and alternates lending sprightly variety.

name: Jumble
classification_notes: A single-weight display face built around thick, curvy strokes with no straight lines or sharp edges, designed explicitly for playful, whimsical contexts such as food packaging and children's products.
summary: A thick, rounded display face with 104 alternates and no two identical counterforms, aimed at food packaging and child-friendly applications.
description: Built entirely from curved strokes with jaunty counters and deliberately varied counterforms, this single-style face avoids straight lines entirely to produce a warm, approachable character. OpenType alternates number 104, plus a handful of ligatures, offering considerable variety within a single weight. Its character suits frosted-cake branding, confectionery packaging and children's game design rather than extended text.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Jumble as a friendly and cute treat for the eyes, drawn with thick, curvy strokes and whimsical counterforms where no two are alike. It includes 104 alternates and a handful of ligatures, and is said to convey humour and warmth without silliness, making it well suited to contexts like frosted cakes, pies, or child-friendly toys and games.

name: Liam
classification_notes: A hand-lettered serif with deliberate baseline bounce, varying slants and curled terminals; its playful, informal construction places it firmly in the display category rather than text serif.
summary: A hand-drawn serif with a bouncing baseline and childlike curls, designed for children's books, toys and similar juvenile applications.
description: Named after the designer's nephew, this single-weight display serif mimics informal hand-lettering through varying slants and curled strokes that lift off the baseline. The family ships with 130 alternates and 52 spot illustrations, giving designers flexibility for decorative layouts. Latin extended coverage spans over two dozen European languages.
foundry_blurb: Liam is described by Laura Worthington as a quirky hand-drawn serif that bounces playfully around the baseline, with cute cowlick curls and varying slants lending childlike charm while retaining legibility. It includes 130 alternates and 52 adorable illustrations, and is recommended for storybooks, toys and other children's products.

name: Mandevilla
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a semi-serif, Mandevilla blends serif and sans-serif letterforms and is designed primarily for titling, display and logo use rather than extended text setting.
summary: A semi-serif display family with an elaborate decorative uppercase set, 210 swashes and alternates, and 38 ornaments, aimed at titling and logo work.
description: Mandevilla offers two distinct uppercase registers: a decorative default set with ornamental caps, and a plain titling set for less embellished applications. A 3/4-size small caps set, which drops the serifs and reduces ornamentation, allows mixed-case settings to retain coherent word shapes. Eight stylistic sets give fine control over letterform variation without requiring professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Mandevilla as a semi-serif ideal for titling, display and logos, featuring 210 swashes and alternates, a decorative default uppercase alongside an unadorned titling set, 3/4-size serif-free small caps, and 38 complementing ornaments. All OpenType features are accessible without specialist software.

name: Modish
classification_notes: A hand-drawn casual script with ink-style strokes and extensive swash and alternate glyph sets, designed to mimic natural handwriting while remaining digitally polished.
summary: A casual ink-style script with 270 swashes and 78 stylistic alternates, aimed at packaging, menus and retail branding.
description: Balancing a hand-drawn feel with digital precision, Modish offers both connected and unconnected letterform options through its extensive set of contextual alternates and stylistic sets. The single Regular style includes 43 ligatures and titling capitals, and the OpenType features are coded to work without professional design software. Broad Latin Extended language support covers over 25 languages across Central and Northern Europe.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Modish as a balanced synthesis of the casually hand-drawn and the pixel-perfect slickness of digital design — inky but neat, flowing but controlled. She positions it for on-trend work in food, clothing, cosmetics, retail packaging and menus, and highlights its 270 swashes, 78 stylistic alternates, contextual variants and 43 ligatures.

name: Novantique Script
classification_notes: A flowing script with multiple stylistic sets and formal letterform structure, positioned within an editorial type system alongside serif and sans companions.
summary: A formal script in three weights, designed as part of the broader Novantique four-family editorial type system.
description: Part of the 22-style Novantique collection alongside Sans, Serif Display and Serif companions, the Script is offered in Light, Regular and Bold. Extensive OpenType support includes 20 stylistic sets, common ligatures, small capitals and fractions, giving considerable flexibility across editorial contexts. Broad Latin Extended coverage spans over 25 European languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Novantique as a captivating collection of four distinct yet visually related families, with all 22 styles together representing elegance and modernity. The families are designed to work individually or in combination across front covers, headlines, sections, ledes, text and bylines.

name: Nelson
classification_notes: Nelson is a capitals-focused display family evoking painted wood signage and Old West lettering, with engraved, ornamental, rugged and bold variants designed for large-scale use.
summary: A capitals-first display family evoking painted and engraved wood signage of the American Old West, offered in four stylistic variants.
description: Nelson draws on the visual language of weathered wood and hand-painted lettering, producing confident all-caps letterforms suited to wordmarks, food packaging and large-format signage. The family ships in four variants: Engraved, Ornaments, Rugged and Bold, with four optically-optimised size grades. OpenType swashes, alternates and ornaments are coded for access without specialist software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Nelson as evocative of paint on weathered wood, with capital letters as rustic and confident as the Old West. The family offers four optically-optimised variants spanning large to small, and is positioned for wordmarks, food packaging, restaurant menus and roadside attraction signage.

name: Novantique Sans
classification_notes: The sans-serif member of the Novantique editorial collection, designed for use alongside serif and script companions in layered typographic systems; its humanist sensibility suits headline and text roles in magazine contexts.
summary: A sans-serif designed as part of the four-family Novantique collection, covering three weights with italics for editorial use from covers to body text.
description: One of four related families in the Novantique collection alongside a script, a serif display and a text serif, this sans runs from Light to Bold with matching italics across six styles. OpenType features include small capitals, stylistic sets, fractions and common ligatures, giving it solid typographic utility for editorial hierarchies. Broad Latin Extended coverage handles the majority of Western European languages.
foundry_blurb: Novantique is a collection of four visually related families totalling 22 styles across Sans, Script, Serif Display and Serif. Worthington describes the collection as representing elegance and modernity, suited to editorial use across front covers, headlines, sections, ledes, text and bylines, and designed to work individually or in combination.

name: Number Five
classification_notes: A connected casual script with an informal, hand-lettered character evoking mid-century American signage and lettering traditions. The availability of unconnected letter alternates does not alter its primary classification as a casual script.
summary: A bold casual script in two textures — Rough and Smooth — drawing on 1940s and 1950s American hand-lettering traditions for titling and display use.
description: Offered in Rough and Smooth variants, this connected script carries the easy confidence of mid-century American signage and editorial lettering. A large alternate library of 433 glyphs includes unconnected letter options, swash capitals and 10 ornaments, giving considerable flexibility for logo and display applications. OpenType features are coded to remain accessible without professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Number Five as pure Americana, suited to titling, display, logo, signage and editorial work. The bold, casual lettering evokes the spirit of 1940s and 1950s America, and the family ships with 433 alternates including an unconnected letter set alongside the default connected script, plus 10 ornaments.

name: Novantique Serif
classification_notes: A text and display serif family with clear distinction between text and display optical sizes, designed for layered editorial use across body copy, headlines and covers.
summary: A 13-style serif family split into text and display optical sizes, designed for layered editorial work from body copy through to front covers.
description: Novantique Serif offers two optically distinct sub-families: a text-optimised family in Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics, and a Display family that adds a swash variant for decorative headline use. The family supports a broad range of Latin-based European languages and includes extensive OpenType features including small capitals, titling alternates and stylistic sets. It is part of the wider Novantique collection, which also includes Sans, Script and a second Serif family, all designed to work together across a layered editorial system.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Novantique as a captivating collection of four distinct yet visually related families — Sans, Script, Serif Display and Serif — representing elegance and modernity across 22 styles. Designed for richly layered editorials, the families can be used individually or in combination across front covers, headlines, sections, ledes, text and bylines.

name: Origins
classification_notes: Origins is drawn from crow quill calligraphy on parchment, with angular letterforms, swash capitals and ornamental characters — firmly a calligraphic script typeface with an antique character.
summary: A crow quill calligraphic script with angular energy and an extensive set of swashes, alternates and ornaments suited to food, wine and occasion-based branding.
description: Drawn by hand on parchment using a crow quill, Origins brings tight angular letterforms together with swooping swash capitals, alternate ascenders and descenders, and purpose-built ending characters. The family ships in two variants — Regular and Smooth — and includes 120 alternates and swashes, 8 ligatures and 20 ornaments, all accessible without professional design software. It suits applications in food and drink, travel, celebrations and heritage-themed work.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Origins as rooted in hand-drawn letterforms made with a crow quill on parchment, giving it calligraphic grace and an antique atmosphere. Its tight, energetic angularity can be paired with swooping swash capitals, alternate ascending and descending letterforms, and graceful ending characters. The foundry notes it performs well in contexts related to food and wine, celebrations, travel and history, and highlights that the fonts are specially coded for accessing all swashes and alternates without the need for professional design software.

name: Regina
classification_notes: Regina is an upright, broad-stroked handwritten script with flowing, connected letterforms typical of a casual calligraphic hand, placing it firmly in the script category.
summary: A broad, upright handwritten script designed for display use on invitations, menus and letters, with wide language coverage across Latin-based scripts.
description: Regina draws on the unhurried quality of handpenned correspondence, with languorous letterforms that retain strong legibility at both display and text sizes. A single Regular weight keeps the offering simple, suited to stationery, menus and invitations where a warm, personal tone is needed. Language support spans over 25 Latin-based languages including Czech, Polish, Turkish and Romanian.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Regina as a broad, welcoming and highly legible script whose languorous letterforms evoke an era of carefully handpenned correspondence. The foundry recommends it for display or text use on letters, invitations and menus.

name: Renata
classification_notes: Renata is a connected script drawn with a small brush-tipped pen, featuring calligraphic letterforms with beginning, ending and isolated forms, placing it firmly in the brush script subcategory.
summary: A single-weight brush script with a high x-height, 119 swashes and per-letter alternates including initial, final and isolated forms for natural-looking connected lettering.
description: Drawn with a small brush-tipped pen, the letterforms sit between casual and upscale, with spacious connecting strokes that aid legibility at typical display sizes. Every lowercase letter ships with an alternate plus beginning, ending and isolated forms, making it well suited to wordmarks, lettered lists and phrases where letters appear detached. OpenType features are coded to work without professional design software, broadening access to the full glyph set.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Renata as both casual and upscale, with languorous letterforms stroked by an experienced calligrapher using a small brush-tipped pen. The typeface offers a high x-height and spacious connecting strokes for readability, with every lowercase letter including an alternate plus beginning, ending and isolated forms, and 119 swashes for a custom look.

name: Recherche
classification_notes: Recherche is based on italic, calligraphic letterforms with long arcing verticals and oval counters, firmly placing it in the script/calligraphic category. Its extensive swash alternates reinforce its decorative, handwritten character.
summary: A calligraphic italic script with long arcing verticals and 89 lowercase swash alternates, suited to menus, wine labels and wedding stationery.
description: Built on italic, calligraphic letterforms with oval counters and airy proportions, Recherche leans into decorative elegance through a full set of swash capitals and 89 lowercase swash alternates. The OpenType features are accessible without professional design software, making it practical for a wide range of users. Available as a single Regular style, it is aimed at display and short-form settings where stylistic variety matters more than weight range.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Recherche as embodying 'studied refinement or elegance', with calligraphic letterforms, long arking verticals and oval counters that convey airy sophistication. The font is packed with swashy alternates including a full set of swash capitals and 89 lowercase swashes, making it easy to create unique settings for menus, wine labels, wedding invitations and more. The fonts are specially coded so all swashes, alternates and ornaments are accessible without professional design software.

name: Rosarian
classification_notes: Rosarian is a connected script derived from pointed brush lettering, characterised by thick downstrokes and thin hairlines typical of the pointed brush tradition.
summary: A pointed brush script in two weights with extensive OpenType features designed to simulate hand-lettered variation.
description: Built on the rhythm of the pointed brush, Rosarian moves between thick rounded downstrokes and thin hairlines to produce a fluid, calligraphic feel. Contextual alternates shift letterforms automatically to avoid repetition, while multiple stylistic sets, swashes and unconnected alternates let the script shift between formal and casual registers. The family has been coded so that alternates and ornaments are accessible without specialist design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Rosarian as a celebration of the pointed brush, flowing expressively from thick, rounded downstrokes to delicate hairlines. Contextual alternates make it read convincingly as hand lettering, while stylistic alternates allow it to be used as a less formal, unconnected script. The fonts are coded for easy access to swashes, alternates and ornaments without professional design software.

name: Samantha
classification_notes: Based explicitly on pointed-pen lettering with flowing letterforms, swash characters and calligraphic alternates, placing it firmly in the script category with a pointed-pen subcategory.
summary: A pointed-pen script with slightly condensed proportions and an extensive set of over 1,100 alternates, swashes and ornaments across upright and italic variants.
description: Built on pointed-pen lettering, Samantha comes in four styles combining regular and bold weights with upright and italic cuts. The family ships with more than 1,100 alternate and swash characters varying in size and complexity, alongside 60 ornaments, 45 catchwords, lining and oldstyle numerals, and 20 stylistic sets. Specially coded for access to all swash and alternate glyphs without specialist design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Samantha as a bright and cheerful font based on pointed-pen lettering, with slightly condensed characters and a measured rhythm. It includes upright and italic variants in regular and bold weights, over 1,100 alternates and swash characters, 60 ornaments, 45 catchwords, and lining, oldstyle and swash numerals.

name: Rosebriar
classification_notes: Rosebriar is a decorative display typeface built on a roman structure but enriched with gothic ornamental details such as wedge cross strokes, splayed terminals and diamond-shaped tittles, placing it firmly in the ornamental display category with a gothic-foliate subcategory.
summary: A single-weight display face that grafts gothic ornamental details onto a roman skeleton, producing something that can read medieval, storybook or simply crafted depending on context.
description: Wedge cross strokes, splayed terminals and diamond-shaped tittles give Rosebriar a foliate, handcrafted quality without committing fully to blackletter. The roman foundation keeps it legible at titling sizes, and stylistic alternates add flexibility for headlines, subheadings and short callouts. Latin Extended coverage spans more than two dozen languages including Czech, Polish and Turkish.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Rosebriar as a decorative display typeface with a clear personality: ornamental, slightly nostalgic, and full of character without locking you into a single look. Gothic ornamental details applied selectively to a roman structure give it a crafted, foliate quality that can read as medieval, storybook or simply distinct, with enough practicality for subheads and callouts beyond pure titling use.

name: Sepian
classification_notes: Described explicitly as an update on the textura blackletter form, with sharp angular strokes characteristic of the gothic/textura tradition.
summary: A sharp, contemporary take on the textura blackletter form, pitched at tattoo, horror and gaming applications.
description: Built on the centuries-old textura structure, Sepian sharpens the form into something with a distinctly dark, graphic edge. A single Regular style is available, coded to expose swashes, alternates and ornaments without requiring professional design software. Language support spans a broad range of Latin-based European languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Sepian as a wickedly fresh update on the textura blackletter, citing its razor-sharp gothic face and darkly cool character as suited to tattoos, horror-movie posters and scary video game text. The font is specially coded for easy access to swashes, alternates and ornaments.

name: Sheila
classification_notes: A script that blends casual handwriting with calligraphic forms, featuring contextual alternates, ligatures and swash letters typical of the brush-influenced script category.
summary: A two-weight script balancing casual handwriting with calligraphic structure, featuring 35 swash forms and contextual alternates for natural-looking connections.
description: Designed for friendly or feminine applications such as inspirational quotes and fashion layouts, it combines airy letterforms with careful calligraphic construction. Contextual alternates and ligatures create natural word flow, while 35 swash forms provide decorative variety. The family includes special coding to make swashes, alternates and ornaments accessible without professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Sheila as striking the perfect balance between casual handwriting and careful calligraphy, making it both approachable and aspirational. She highlights its airy, breezy letterforms as suited to friendly or feminine settings, and notes that contextual alternates, ligatures and 35 swash forms give the typeface a natural, distinctive character.

name: Samantha Craft
classification_notes: Based explicitly on pointed-pen lettering with a slightly condensed, rhythmic structure, placing it firmly in the pointed-pen script tradition alongside calligraphic brush and copperplate styles.
summary: A pointed-pen script with slightly condensed letterforms, a measured rhythm and an extensive OpenType feature set including swashes, ornaments and 20 stylistic sets.
description: Drawn from pointed-pen lettering traditions, Samantha Craft has a bright, upright quality with a slightly condensed structure that keeps it legible at display sizes. The family ships in two styles, Craft and Frames, with a full suite of OpenType features including swashes, titling capitals, discretionary ligatures and 20 stylistic sets. Laura Worthington has coded the fonts so that swashes and alternates are accessible without professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Samantha Craft as a bright and cheerful font based on pointed-pen lettering, featuring slightly condensed characters and a measured rhythm. The fonts are specially coded to give access to all swashes, alternates and ornaments without the need for professional design software.

name: Tangelo
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a casual display script with unicase characteristics, mixing lowercase letterforms scaled to cap height, placing it firmly in the casual script category.
summary: A casual unicase display script with 115 alternates and swash characters, aimed at packaging, games and children's products.
description: Tangelo blends lowercase letterforms scaled to cap height, creating a unicase character that suits playful, informal applications. The 115 alternates and swash characters give it a convincingly hand-lettered quality without manual glyph substitution, as the fonts are coded for access outside professional design software. Two weights, Regular and Bold, cover Latin Extended across 27 languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Tangelo as a fun, casual display script that is more accurately a unicase, borrowing characters from the lowercase and scaling them to cap size. She positions it for kid-friendly uses such as candy packaging, game boards, apps and clothing labels, and highlights 115 alternates and swash characters that can be accessed without professional design software.

name: Spumante
classification_notes: A semi-connected, upright script with lithely curved letterforms, placing it firmly in the script category with an upright rather than slanted axis, closer to copperplate-influenced display lettering.
summary: A slim, semi-connected upright script with over 200 swashes, alternates and ornaments, suited to packaging, labels and invitations.
description: Spumante's upright curves and semi-connected letterforms give it a sprightly, decorative character without the forward lean typical of calligraphic scripts. The family includes a shadow variant and a bold weight, while titling alternates produce an unconnected, minimalist look when required. Extensive OpenType support covers stylistic sets, ornaments and common ligatures, accessible even without professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Spumante as a slim, semi-connected script with lithely upright curves, ideal for food packaging and menus, cosmetic labels, book covers, and greeting cards or invitations. The family can be dressed up with over 200 swashes, alternates and ornaments, or stripped back using titling alternates for a minimalist, unconnected appearance.

name: Shelby
classification_notes: Shelby is a monoline, semi-connected script based on hand lettering, giving it the casual, informal character of handwriting. Its fluid, connected letterforms place it firmly in the script category, with a relaxed handwritten subcategory.
summary: A monoline, semi-connected handwritten script in two weights, with ligatures, stylistic alternates and 20 ornaments for headlines, logos and call-outs.
description: Shelby is a casual script drawn from hand lettering, with a monoline stroke and semi-connected letterforms that keep it legible at display sizes. The family ships in Regular and Bold, with common ligatures, stylistic and contextual alternates, and 20 complementary ornaments accessible without specialist design software. Language support spans over 25 Latin-based languages including French, German, Polish and Turkish.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Shelby as friendly and casual, a monoline semi-connected script based on hand lettering. It is designed for headlines, logos and eye-catching call-outs, and includes ligatures, stylistic and contextual alternates, and 20 ornaments to complement its handwritten character, all coded for access without professional design software.

name: Tiva
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a casual script reminiscent of handwriting, with a light and breezy character and extensive alternates typical of retail script typefaces.
summary: A light, casual handwriting script with a wide set of contextual and stylistic alternates for adding a personal tone to printed and digital projects.
description: Built around an informal, flowing hand, Tiva offers a single Regular weight with extensive OpenType support including common ligatures, stylistic alternates and stylistic sets. The foundry has coded the font for access to swashes and ornaments without requiring professional design software, broadening its usability for non-specialist workflows. Latin coverage spans 27 languages across Central and Northern Europe.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Tiva as a light and breezy casual script reminiscent of handwriting, packed with contextual and stylistic alternates, and designed to add a personal touch to any project.

name: Voltage
classification_notes: Voltage is a connected script with structured, upright letterforms referencing industrial metal automobile lettering, giving it a mechanical quality distinct from calligraphic or brush scripts.
summary: A structured connected script inspired by the embossed metal lettering found on automobiles of the early-to-mid 20th century.
description: Drawing on the vernacular of Industrial Age automobile badging, Voltage sits between script and mechanical lettering with a uniformity that sets it apart from looser calligraphic styles. The family spans three weights and ships with an unusually generous set of OpenType features: 154 swash designs with 348 total variations, 39 alternates and 15 ligatures. Laura Worthington has also coded the fonts to make all swashes and alternates accessible without specialist design software.
foundry_blurb: Worthington describes Voltage as an unexpected and energetic standout among script fonts, evoking the metal lettering on vintage automobiles and referencing the late Industrial Age. Its structured letterforms emphasise practicality and uniformity whilst remaining assertive and down-to-earth, and the family includes 154 unique swash designs, 39 alternates and 15 ligatures with special coding for easy access to all features.

name: Theodosia
classification_notes: Theodosia is explicitly described as a roundhand-style script, drawing from the long, flowing lines of calligraphy tradition with a light, airy quality.
summary: A roundhand script by Laura Worthington offered in three widths and as a variable font, designed to expand and contract without distortion.
description: Inspired by the long, clean lines of calligraphy, this airy roundhand script comes in Condensed, Regular and Expanded static styles, plus a variable font with a width axis spanning all three. The three-width approach was built into the design from the outset, avoiding the optical distortion that typically results from artificially squeezing or stretching a single master. It suits invitations, packaging, book jackets and magazine layouts where a light, flowing script is needed with some flexibility in setting width.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Theodosia as an ethereal roundhand-style script with the feeling of the coming and going of the tide, inspired by the long, languid, clean lines of calligraphy. Designed to be light as air, it adds a distinguishing pattern and texture to invitations, packaging, brand design, magazine spreads and book jackets. She intentionally designed it to expand and contract without manipulation or distortion, available in Regular, Condensed and Expanded or as a variable font.

name: Winter Wallflowers
classification_notes: Winter Wallflowers is a decorative symbol font comprising hand-drawn wallpaper tiles and icons rather than conventional letterforms, placing it firmly in the ornamental display category.
summary: A hand-drawn symbol font of 25 winter-themed wallpaper tiles and icons that generate repeating borders and patterns when typed consecutively.
description: Each character maps to a hand-drawn wallpaper tile or accompanying icon, so typing a run of the same letter produces a seamless decorative border or repeating pattern. The approach requires no design software beyond a text cursor, making it accessible for greeting cards, wrapping paper or seasonal branding. Coverage is limited to Latin script with a modest set of supported languages.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Winter Wallflowers as a tool for creating borders, wallpaper and repeating patterns using 25 unique hand-drawn wallpaper tiles and accompanying icons. Users simply type the same letter consecutively to produce a pattern, making decorative borders straightforward to construct.

name: Wallflowers
classification_notes: Wallflowers is a decorative typeface family built around hand-drawn botanical tiles and icons, functioning as an ornamental and pattern-making tool rather than a conventional text face.
summary: A hand-drawn botanical dingbat family in three styles, designed for constructing borders, wallpaper patterns and repeating decorative motifs.
description: Three companion styles make up the family, with Wallflowers III offering hand-drawn wallpaper tiles and icons that tile seamlessly when characters are typed consecutively. The character set covers basic Latin letters mapped to individual pattern units, making it straightforward to build decorative borders or repeating backgrounds without any illustration software. Language support is modest, reflecting the ornamental rather than typographic purpose of the family.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington Design describes Wallflowers as a tool for creating borders, wallpaper and repeating patterns using the unique hand-drawn tiles in Wallflowers III, with instructions to type the same letter consecutively to generate a continuous pattern.

name: Winsome
classification_notes: Winsome is a connected script that blends casual handwritten qualities with calligraphic influences, placing it firmly in the casual script subcategory.
summary: A casual connected script with extensive OpenType customisation including 466 swash characters, 89 ligatures and 59 alternates.
description: Generous letter spacing and unfussy letterforms give this script an approachable, readable quality that sits between casual handwriting and formal calligraphy. A titling feature unlocks a disconnected version for added design flexibility. The OpenType suite has been coded for access without professional design software.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Winsome as a friendly script offering virtually endless variation, balancing the casual with the calligraphic. It includes over 59 alternates, 466 swash characters and 89 ligatures, with a disconnected titling variant for extra design versatility. The fonts are specially coded so all swashes, alternates and ornaments are accessible without professional software.

name: Yana
classification_notes: Yana is described as having classical proportions, suggesting a traditional serif design suited to both text and display settings; the inclusion of swash capitals and small caps places it within a decorative classical serif tradition.
summary: A classical serif with swash capitals and small caps, offered in three styles for text and titling work.
description: Yana comes in Regular, Regular Italic and Bold, with classical proportions aimed at both extended text and headline settings. The family includes two sets of swash capitals, each available in standard, small cap and petite cap sizes, giving designers scope to add calligraphic flourishes to wordmarks and editorial headings. OpenType features cover old-style numerals, titling capitals, stylistic alternates and superscript, with broad Latin Extended language support.
foundry_blurb: Laura Worthington describes Yana as classy and confident, with classical proportions that convey sophistication. Designed for versatility across text, headlines and titling, the family includes two sets of swash capitals available in standard, small cap and petite cap sizes, and has been coded for access to swashes, alternates and ornaments without the need for professional design software.
